nativeRange.habitat: Streams, rivers, and marshes in tropical rainforest understory; grows attached to wood, roots, and rocks at the water margin, often emersed

decorPreferences: shaded areas to reduce leaf algae

goodWith: African cichlids (leaves are tough enough to resist most nibbling)

summary: A hardy, slow-growing West African rhizome plant with tough dark-green leaves. Generally considered beginner-friendly; the rhizome must be attached to hardscape and never buried.
careNotes: Anubias barteri is a robust, slow-growing epiphytic rhizome plant from West African streams and rainforest margins. It is one of the most beginner-friendly aquarium plants and tolerates a wide range of conditions, but it has one critical care requirement: the rhizome (the thick horizontal stem from which leaves and roots emerge) MUST NOT BE BURIED in substrate. Burying the rhizome causes it to rot and the plant to melt and die. Instead, attach the plant to driftwood, rocks, or other hardscape using thread, fishing line, or a small dab of cyanoacrylate (super) glue; the roots will eventually anchor on their own. The leaves are tough and waxy, which means herbivorous fish (including most African cichlids and goldfish-adjacent species) tend to leave it alone — although goldfish and very large plant-eaters can still damage it. Anubias grows slowly, so leaves can accumulate algae; placement in shaded areas of the tank (under taller plants, behind hardscape, or in lower light zones) significantly reduces algae buildup on the leaf surfaces. Lighting is flexible: low-to-medium light is preferred and produces vibrant growth without excessive algae; higher light accelerates growth but increases algae risk. CO2 is not required but is reported to improve growth and leaf quality when injected. Regular liquid fertilization is beneficial; root tabs are less critical since most nutrient uptake is via the rhizome and roots in the water column rather than substrate. Avoid drastic parameter changes — sudden swings can trigger melt. Temperature range based on consensus across Modern Aquarium and Aquarium Care Basics (22–28 °C); Tropica does not state a numerical range. pH range 6.0–7.8 from Buce Plant and Aquarium Care Basics; sources note the plant adapts to 6.0–8.0. gH range 2–15 from secondary aquarium hobby sources; kH is not reliably stated. Trade-name note: this entry covers the standard 'Anubias barteri var. barteri' (the broad-leaf form most commonly sold simply as 'Anubias Barteri' or 'Anubias'); numerous cultivars and varieties exist (var. nana, 'Coffeefolia', 'Petite', var. caladiifolia, var. glabra, 'Broad Leaf', 'Round / Golden Coin', 'Butterfly', 'Striped', 'Dark Angel'). Most varieties share identical care; the main differences are leaf shape and adult size (var. nana stays much smaller at 5–10 cm).
breedingNotes: Propagated by rhizome division: with a sharp blade, cut a healthy rhizome into sections, ensuring each section retains at least 3–4 healthy leaves and some attached roots. Re-attach each section to hardscape — do not bury. Anubias can also flower underwater, producing a small white spathe-and-spadix inflorescence typical of the Araceae family, but seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Primary source page for Anubias barteri var. barteri. Confirmed origin (West Africa), height (25–45 cm), low light demand, low CO2 demand, slow growth, easy difficulty, and the critical rhizome-must-not-be-buried instruction.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked care difficulty (easy), light (low), CO2 (not necessary), pH (6.0–7.5), and rhizome-handling guidance. Buce Plant explicitly warns that burying the rhizome causes melt and death; sourced propagation method (rhizome division).

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Used for temperature range (22–28 °C / 72–82 °F), pH tolerance (6.0–8.0), shaded-placement guidance to reduce leaf algae, and confirmation of rhizome attachment to hardscape vs. burying.

additional.name: Aquarium Care Basics
additional.notes: Used for pH range confirmation (6.5–7.8), temperature confirmation (22–26 °C), gH range (2–15), and substrate / planting notes.
